reason: ValueError: not enough values to unpack (expected 3, got 2) at transformers/modeling_llama.py:741 — LlamaDecoderLayer expects self_attn to return 3 values (hidden_states, self_attn_weights, present_key_value) but custom attention only returns 2. This is a code bug in the OOM-optimized attention implementation.
SparseMemoryAttention patching incompatible with transformers output_capturing.py - crash at first decoder_layer forward call

fix: SparseMemoryAttention.forward() was returning 3 values (output, None, None) but transformers 5.x LlamaDecoderLayer expects 2 (attn_output, attn_weights) — unpacks as `hidden_states, _ = self.self_attn(...)`. Changed return to 2-tuple. File: src/memory/sparse_memory/attention.py

reason: ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 2) — SparseMemoryAttention returns 3-tuple, LlamaDecoderLayer expects 2

command: NUM_GPUS=8 BATCH_SIZE=2 SEQ_LEN=4096 MEMORY_SLOTS=128 bash scripts/run_train_sparse_memory.sh
reason: 2-tuple return fix from SparseMemoryAttention is confirmed to work. Training proceeded past the attention layer without unpack error. Execution reached decoder_layer -> input_layernorm. The only remaining issue is OOM (94.89 GiB / 95 GiB per GPU) which requires hyperparameter tuning (batch_size, seq_len) or switching to FSDP/DeepSpeed instead of DDP. This is a config change that requires main approval.

command: NUM_GPUS=8 BATCH_SIZE=1 SEQ_LEN=2048 MEMORY_SLOTS=128 MAX_STEPS=200 bash scripts/run_train_sparse_memory.sh
reason: PreTokenizedDataset.__post_init__ calls np.load() on DATA_PATH (default: pg19_train.jsonl), but that's a JSONL file, not a .npy file. np.load rejects it with 'ValueError: This file contains pickled (object) data'. The script requires a pre-tokenized .npy file of shape [N, seq_len]. Available: data/wikitext_tokenized_1024.npy (seq_len=1024, incompatible with seq_len=2048). Fix options: (1) generate a .npy file for seq_len=2048, (2) change the dataset class to load .jsonl, (3) use seq_len=1024. All require code/data changes that need main approval.
